commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2016e11acd64c95ea92ad6e3ef03342a8a7480ed)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex app-server prompt guidance and projector logic to send a one-shot pre-tool `onBlockReply` plan announcement, with tests for fallback copy and event ordering.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a strict bug reproduction: this PR adds a new Codex chat UX behavior. The merge risk is source-reproducible from the current message-tool-only contract and the proposed announcement guard.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or Mantis real behavior proof plus maintainer agreement on the new chat delivery UX; the code issue is clear, but automation cannot supply the contributor proof gate.

target.title: Announce tool plans in chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds Codex-runtime developer guidance so chat turns that will need tools first emit a one- or two-sentence, chat-ready acknowledgement in the plan explanation before the first tool call.
- Makes the app-server event projector prefer that natural plan explanation over mechanical plan-step or tool-name fallback copy when sending the pre-tool chat reply back through the original channel medium.
- Keeps deterministic redacted fallback copy only for cases where Codex has not produced a usable plan explanation.

## Root Cause

Before this patch, OpenClaw could only build the pre-tool chat reply from deterministic fallback signals such as tool names or plan step labels. That made the first reply accurate about tool usage, but still too generic for real operator asks because the projector ignored the model's plan explanation and the Codex runtime prompt did not require a chat-ready, request-specific acknowledgement.

Concrete before behavior: for a monitoring request like checking Beszel and Uptime Kuma, the old path could produce generic copy such as a step list or a tool-action statement instead of saying what system health it was about to inspect. Inspected code evidence: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts` built the announcement only from `latestPlanAnnouncementSteps` or tool-call details, and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` had no Codex-runtime instruction telling the model to put the exact chat acknowledgement into the first plan explanation.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a tool-requiring chat request now gets a specific, persona-shaped acknowledgement in the same chat medium before tool progress starts.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw temp worktree with the Codex app-server projector and Codex developer-instruction builder.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a manual projector probe that built Codex developer instructions, sent a `turn/plan/updated` event whose explanation was `I'll check your Beszel and Uptime Kuma instances to make sure nothing looks dead or dying.`, then sent the first tool `item/started` event.

Evidence after fix: the developer instructions include the Beszel/Uptime Kuma example and `SOUL.md` guidance, and the projector emitted `onBlockReply` with exactly `I'll check your Beszel and Uptime Kuma instances to make sure nothing looks dead or dying.` before the tool-start events.

Observed result after fix: the first visible chat reply is specific to the user's monitoring ask and precedes the first tool progress event; it is not a generic “use tools” or “continue the request” statement.

What was not tested: no live Telegram group-topic roundtrip against real Beszel/Uptime Kuma was run in this patch; no autoreview was run per operator instruction.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — 3 files passed, 286 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json` — extension and extension-test lanes only
- `oc-update --skip-codex` — completed after applying PR #84534 as an overlay; local health/security checks reported unrelated operator-environment warnings that are not caused by this patch
